title: Fresh-tyre next-stop finish feasibility
summary: Model whether a pit stop on the next lap would put the car on tyres capable of reaching the flag.
rationale: The strongest observed idea family was the complementary view to current-tyre pressure: not only whether the current tyre is exhausted, but whether a next-lap stop creates a feasible final stint. This directly maps to pit-window decision logic.
implementation_hint: Compute estimated laps remaining after a next-lap stop and compare it with fixed fresh-life windows for SOFT, MEDIUM, HARD, INTERMEDIATE, and WET. Add any-fresh-can-finish, any-fresh-slick-can-finish, best fresh margin, and stop-solves-current-pressure indicators.
expected_effect: Medium expected ROC AUC gain with high confidence; it was consistently useful and compact.
risk: Approximate life windows may be race-dependent; keep both class-level flags and continuous margins to reduce threshold overfit.
